items.summary: Tourism businesses say they’re bearing the brunt of surging fuel prices, with some losing thousands to booking cancellations and extra operational costs, while others field back-to-back calls from anxious tourists about fuel availability.

items.title: Forestry company parks electric truck despite spike in diesel prices
items.summary: A 2.5-year trial of an electric truck in SA's forestry industry has concluded with the truck now parked, finding it was able to do the job but wasn't cost effective, even with high fuel prices.

items.title: Artemis II crew appear to be in high spirits ahead of moon mission
items.summary: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

items.title: Astronauts enter space capsule ahead of Artemis II blast-off
items.summary: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

items.title: Sixers coach Mike Wells denies disrespecting Kings' Matthew Dellavedova in NBL championship series
items.summary: Adelaide 36ers coach Mike Wells rejects an accusation he has shown a lack of respect to Sydney Kings' Matthew Dellavedova during the NBL championship series.

items.title: Keir Starmer and European leaders defy Donald Trump as cost of war hits home
items.summary: Early on, world leaders went out of their way to placate Donald Trump. But as the war with Iran drags into a second month, the UK and European leaders are finding their voices.

items.title: Daly River and Palumpa flood evacuees transferred to Batchelor, doubling town's population overnight
items.summary: Flood evacuees from Daly River and Palumpa have been transferred from Darwin to the small town of Batchelor, where they could stay for months. Locals say they were not consulted before the town's population doubled overnight.

items.title: Bumper season brings cheaper avocados, 'record' exports, but growers pay price
items.summary: A bumper avocado season is creating a perfect storm for cheaper produce and record export volumes, but growers are doing it tough.

items.title: Look back on the launch of NASA's first Moon mission in half a century
items.summary: Four astronauts are on board the Orion capsule, orbiting Earth as humanity seeks to return to the Moon for the first time in 54 years. Take a look back at how the day's events unfolded.

items.title: England's Harry Brook and Jacob Bethell let off with warning over New Zealand nightclub altercation
items.summary: Harry Brook and Jacob Bethell escape with a caution notice following an investigation into a nightclub incident in ‌New Zealand late ⁠last year.

items.title: As it happened: ASX drops on back of Trump speech, oil back up
items.summary: ASX drops back following President Donald Trump's speech on Iran. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News markets blog.

items.title: Federal government threatens to use powers to limit gas exports unless producers preserve enough for local use
items.summary: It is the first time the government has taken a step towards using the powers it has to limit exports, and it comes at a time when the profits from those exports appear set to surge.

items.title: Outback GPs warn delays in patient data across services put lives at risk
items.summary: Rural GPs say privacy barriers and fragmented systems mean vital patient information can take over a month to access, putting patients at risk.

items.title: Sameer Rizvi's Capitals post six-wicket IPL win over Mitch Marsh's Super Giants
items.summary: Mitch Marsh launches his Indian Premier League season with three big sixes, but it was not enough to help the Lucknow Super Giants in a six-wicket loss to Delhi Capitals.

items.title: Dezi Freeman's time on the run puts sovereign citizens in the spotlight
items.summary: As detectives investigate whether the sovereign citizen community helped harbour police killer Dezi Freeman, researchers say the anti-government movement appears to be surging in regional Australia.

items.title: Ikara-Flinders Ranges Easter tourism 'worse than COVID', while other regional towns see spike
items.summary: An Ikara-Flinders Ranges tourism operator says he is experiencing more cancellations than bookings this Easter as fuel shortage anxiety keep travellers away, while other regional towns expect to be booked out.

items.title: Sydney Royal Easter Show to go on, despite rising fuel costs
items.summary: The country has come to the city for the Royal Easter Show and despite the cost of the trip blowing out amid high fuel prices, the exhibitors say they would not miss it.

items.title: Forecast of calm Easter weather for most of Australia, but another cyclone looms
items.summary: Australia has just been soaked by its third wettest February-March on record, but for most of the country, skies have cleared up just in time for Easter.

items.title: Australia wasting migrant talent on an 'industrial scale', former Treasury secretary says
items.summary: Australia is failing to recognise the qualifications of highly trained permanent migrants, former Treasury secretary Martin Parkinson says.

items.title: Singapore's major oil source is blocked and experts warn Australians will pay
items.summary: Singapore is exposed to the oil shock from Iran's effective closure of the Strait of Hormuz, and its response will shape the fuel crisis in Australia.

items.title: US burning through years' worth of critical weapons stockpiles in Iran
items.summary: Analysts estimate the US is burning through high-end weapons in Iran at a rate that could take years to replenish, risking the West's military readiness for other potential conflicts.

items.title: What Australia can learn from France's approach to free public transport
items.summary: There are growing calls in Australia for free public transport amid the Iran war with two states putting in place free services on a temporary basis. But in France, almost 50 cities have free public transport.

items.title: One Nation picked candidates accused of domestic abuse, antisemitism
items.summary: A One Nation candidate in the South Australian election was accused of domestic abuse, bullying and harassment, while another made racist, antisemitic and homophobic social media posts, raising fresh questions about the party's vetting processes as it surges in popularity.

items.title: The new moonshot
items.summary: If all goes well for NASA's mission, the astronauts aboard could fly the furthest that humans have ever gone. What should we be looking out for?

items.title: From Children's Laureate to Treehouse on TV, how Andy Griffiths' quirky kids books captured the country
items.summary: Andy Griffiths has written 43 books, is the Children's Laureate, and is about to turn his Treehouse series into an ABC TV show. So what inspires the former punk and teacher to keep creating quirky tales for kids?

items.title: Know the difference between a pomologist and an oologist? We have the quiz for you
items.summary: Australia’s museums hold a treasure trove of odd facts, native animals and even burlesque history. So how much do you know about the things hidden in our nation’s vaults?

items.title: Parents demand action after falling concrete closes entire floor of Collingwood College

items.title: Anthony Albanese wants Australians to believe he has a fuel plan
items.summary: With no realistic end in sight and a whole lot of economic pain to come, the PM's decision to make a direct plea to the nation was not one taken lightly.

items.title: At least four killed in daytime Russian drone strikes across Ukraine
items.summary: Both sides are launching drone attacks as the war between Russia and Ukraine drags into its fifth year.

items.title: Adelaide 36ers force NBL title decider in Game 4 cliffhanger against Sydney Kings
items.summary: Torrey Craig's missed free throw for the Sydney Kings in the dying seconds allows the Adelaide 36ers to level the NBL championship series at 2-2 with a gripping 92-91 win.

items.title: Global uncertainty a reason to 'press ahead' with urgent reform, Albanese says
items.summary: The prime minister uses a speech at the National Press Club to make his pitch for "urgent" change amid significant international uncertainty caused by the Middle East war.

items.title: Prime Minister urges Australians to save fuel
items.summary: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

items.title: Five teenagers arrested after multiple home invasions across Brisbane and Gold Coast
items.summary: Multiple people were injured during the series of break-and-enter offences across South Brisbane and the Gold Coast.

items.summary: Carlton head coach Michael Voss has said there is not a "silver bullet" to fix the club's second-half blues, following three successive fade-outs to start the season.

items.summary: The prime minister risks criticism for using the rare measure of a national address to urge calm without announcing any new measures.

items.title: Australian rugby union coach Mick Byrne ends tenure as Fiji boss just 18 months out from World Cup
items.summary: Mick Byrne steps down from his role as head coach of the Fiji national rugby team after two years in charge.

items.title: Albanese urges Australians to 'live life as normal' during fuel crisis in rare national address
items.summary: Anthony Albanese asks Australians to trade their cars for public transport if they can, while reassuring those who need to drive they can continue to do so amid the global oil crisis.

items.summary: News from the world of finance and business. Top stories in text, video and audio. Business video news bulletin. Features and analysis.

items.title: Live Nation's Love Song 2026 finds gen Z embrace retro tech, scrapbooking, in-person connection
items.summary: Gen Z is embracing retro technology like iPods, vinyl and Polaroid cameras and craves in-person connection more than any other generation, according to a Live Nation study.

items.title: Class action alleges GrainCorp had 'expand first, fix problems later' approach to production

items.title: Anthropic boss makes big call on Australian copyright as artists say pay up
items.summary: The AI company's billionaire boss says he is not trying to convince Australia to change its mind on protecting the copyright of artists.

items.title: WA government to use emergency powers to force fuel companies to reveal supply chain information
items.summary: The Western Australian government will use emergency powers to force fuel companies to provide information about their supply chains in an effort to keep fuel flowing through the state.

items.title: Tuesday no longer cheapest day for petrol in Perth as Middle East war disrupts fuel price cycle
items.summary: Perth motorists can no longer rely on "cheap Tuesday" fuel prices and are urged to shop around every day as the Middle East war disrupts global supply chains.

items.title: Graham Arnold coaches Iraq to first FIFA World Cup appearance in 40 years

items.summary: See what's happening with petrol and diesel prices near you, updated constantly.

items.title: Brisbane Broncos senior leaders decider to stand down winger Josiah Karapani

items.title: Kieran Foran adamant age is not an issue ahead of first match as Manly Sea Eagles coach
items.summary: Kieran Foran says he will not be afraid to have hard conversations with former teammates as he prepares to complete the fastest shift from NRL player to head coach this century.

items.title: Daniel's Law child sex offender register website receives more than 290,000 visits in three months
items.summary: Queensland's first public child sex offender register, listing reportable offenders who have failed to comply with their reporting obligations or whose whereabouts are unknown, has been praised for protecting children.

items.title: Court makes landmark decision after illegal logging in NSW state forest
items.summary: Traditional owners say a $450,000 fine will not replace the six centuries-old giant trees illegally felled by NSW Forestry Corporation.

items.summary: The ABC asked how you were coping with fuel prices and here's what we heard back.

items.title: Algal bloom tracks further north up the Yorke Peninsula, sparking concern for stingrays
items.summary: Evidence of South Australia's harmful algal bloom has been detected in previously untouched parts of the Spencer Gulf days after rays were seen gathering in shallow waters.

items.title: Australians expected to generate more solar panel waste each year by 2030, inquiry hears
items.summary: Australians are expected to throw away 90,000 tonnes of solar panel waste each year by 2030, a federal government hearing has been told.

items.title: Zac Lomax to make Super Rugby debut for Western Force in Brisbane

items.summary: Consumers were warned the fuel excise cut might take weeks to be passed on to retailers, but prices have already begun to fall, NRMA says.

items.title: Australia assessing how to protect Pacific nations from fuel shortages caused by Iran war
items.summary: Pacific nations are not yet suffering large-scale petrol shortages but are already starting to grapple with the economic impacts of the war, with fuel price hikes threatening to drive up inflation.

items.title: Mouse plague warning for Western Australia as researcher urges action
items.summary: Grain paddocks in Western Australia are filled with thousands of mice, which are making their way into residential areas.

items.title: Fears for superb fairy-wrens as experts studying Canberra population predict extinction within decades
items.summary: The researchers said the superb fairy-wren would likely go extinct within 30-40 years unless action was taken to stop greenhouse gas emissions causing climate change.

items.title: GOAT is Australian artist Daniel Pozo's first project as a Sony lead
items.summary: Daniel Pozo has had a hand in Sony hits like Spiderman: Across the Spider-Verse and Hotel Transylvania, but GOAT is his first project in the driver's seat.

items.title: Sam Konstas left off Cricket Australia's full contracts list for 2026-27 season
items.summary: Sam Konstas is one of six players left off Cricket Australia's full contracts list for the upcoming season.

items.title: Taylor Swift faces trademark lawsuit over The Life of a Showgirl album
items.summary: Las Vegas performer Maren Wade says the name and imagery of Taylor Swift's latest album are too close to those of her show, Confessions of a Showgirl.

items.title: Anthony Albanese to urge Australians to 'play their part' in Iran response in TV address
items.summary: The prime minister's office says Anthony Albanese will address the nation on TV tonight, urging Australians to save fuel for areas and industries that "most need it".
